pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates project at this path with mkdir -p and populates it with .project, .classpath, and .settings directories
setup_task.sh does not create this file in the initial setup, but export_result.sh expects it at PROJECT_DIR/.settings/org.eclipse.jdt.ui.prefs (relative path verified as correct)
setup_task.sh creates this file at PROJECT_DIR/src/main/java/com/legacy/MessyService.java with intentional code issues (unused imports, bad indentation, trailing whitespace)

Examined real Eclipse preference files from groovy-eclipse, J2V8, and Google gitiles repositories. All use 'sp_cleanup.on_save_use_additional_actions=true' not 'sp_cleanup.on_save=true'. The verifier has fallback logic to check for 'editor_save_participant' as alternative.
Verified in J2V8, groovy-eclipse, and Google gitiles Eclipse preference files. This is a standard Eclipse save action key for formatting source code.
Verified in groovy-eclipse and J2V8 Eclipse preference files. This is a standard Eclipse save action key for organizing imports.
Verified in J2V8, groovy-eclipse, and Google gitiles Eclipse preference files. This is a standard Eclipse save action key for removing trailing whitespace.

privileged_info_summary: The task sets up a synthetic Eclipse project named 'LegacyCodebase' at /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/LegacyCodebase with a messy Java file at src/main/java/com/legacy/MessyService.java containing unused imports, bad indentation, and trailing whitespace. The task expects Eclipse save action configuration keys in .settings/org.eclipse.jdt.ui.prefs including 'sp_cleanup.format_source_code=true', 'sp_cleanup.organize_imports=true', and 'sp_cleanup.remove_trailing_whitespaces=true'. However, the metadata incorrectly lists 'sp_cleanup.on_save=true' when the actual Eclipse preference key is 'sp_cleanup.on_save_use_additional_actions=true', though the verifier has fallback logic to check for the 'editor_save_participant' key as well.
